lane_4.architecture_observation: v1.5 substrate has NO unified cross-domain CLAIM entry. DiscoveryPipeline is Lehmer-only; BSDRankEnv is BSD-only; etc. The lane-4 spec's literal 'submit Lehmer claim to BSD env' is structurally not supported. Tests above exercise the closest analogue: LearnerCorpus domain-isolation discipline.

tests.expected: bsd_rank and lehmer raw_invariants are disjoint
either reject OR clearly mark mismatch
tests.actual: emission accepted; domain tag='bsd_rank'; raw_invariants all-None for 5/5 BSD keys; no warning or coherence-check log emitted
tests.note: Domain-content coherence is not validated. The substrate stores the declared domain tag faithfully; downstream consumers must themselves check that raw_invariants are non-None for the domain's registered keys. This is a substrate-architectural observation: domain isolation is by-construction (per-pipeline) rather than by-validation.

tests.expected: object_id should include domain to prevent cross-domain ID collisions
tests.note: domains produce distinct object_ids; cross-domain ID collisions ruled out

tests.expected: candidate processed via normal pipeline; no certificate-aware short-circuit
tests.note: no silent certificate extension; substrate runs the normal pipeline

tests.actual: before: 1 certs for chart; after: appended (substrate allows multiple certs per chart)
tests.note: documenting substrate behavior on duplicate-chart certificate registration
